title: Delete food entries, 7-day progress chart, calorie limit setting, and exceeded limit alert

requirements.text: Add a deleteFoodEntry(id: Text, date: Text) update function to the backend actor that removes a food entry by its ID for the given date and returns a success/failure result.

quotes: Delete food entries — Add a delete button to each food item in the daily log, with the backend deleteFoodEntry function and immediate recalculation of calories consumed and net balance

acceptanceCriteria: deleteFoodEntry removes the matching entry from stable storage
The daily summary (calories consumed, net calories) reflects the deletion when queried immediately after
Calling deleteFoodEntry with a non-existent ID returns a graceful error result rather than trapping

requirements.text: Add a setCalorieLimit(date: Text, limit: Nat) update function and a getCalorieLimit(date: Text) query function to the backend actor so the daily calorie limit is persisted per date across sessions.

quotes: Calorie limit setting — A persistent, per-day limit stored in the backend so it carries across sessions

acceptanceCriteria: setCalorieLimit stores the limit for the given date in stable storage
getCalorieLimit returns the previously stored limit for that date, or a sensible default (e.g., 2000) if none has been set
The stored limit survives canister upgrades

requirements.text: On the Daily Dashboard, add a delete button (trash icon) to each food entry card. When clicked, call the deleteFoodEntry backend mutation, then immediately invalidate and refresh the daily summary so that total calories consumed and net calorie balance update without a page reload.

quotes: Delete button on each food item in the daily log, with backend deleteFoodEntry function and immediate recalculation of calories consumed and net balance

acceptanceCriteria: Each food entry in the daily log displays a visible delete/trash icon button
Clicking the delete button removes the entry from the list immediately (optimistic or via query invalidation)
Total calories consumed and net calorie balance update to reflect the deletion without requiring a page reload
The delete button shows a loading/disabled state while the mutation is in flight

requirements.text: On the Daily Dashboard, add a calorie limit input control that loads the current day's persisted limit from the backend via getCalorieLimit, allows the user to set or update it (calling setCalorieLimit on save), and displays a 'calories remaining' counter (limit minus calories consumed). When calories consumed exceed the limit, show a prominent in-app visual warning banner or indicator on the dashboard.

quotes: Calorie limit setting — Persistent per-day limit stored in the backend, with a 'calories remaining' display on the dashboard
Exceeded limit alert — In-app visual warning banner when consumed calories exceed the set limit

acceptanceCriteria: A calorie limit input/edit control is visible on the Daily Dashboard, pre-populated with the stored limit for the selected date
Saving a new limit calls setCalorieLimit and immediately refreshes the display
A 'calories remaining' value (limit minus consumed) is shown and updates live as entries are added or deleted
When calories consumed exceed the set limit, a clearly visible warning banner or highlighted indicator appears on the dashboard
The warning disappears when net consumption drops back below the limit

requirements.text: Add a 7-day calorie consumption progress chart to the Daily Dashboard (or a dedicated chart section) that plots daily total calories consumed for the past 7 days as a bar or line chart, with the current day's calorie limit rendered as a horizontal reference line.

quotes: Daily progress chart — Bar/line chart showing calorie consumption for the past 7 days, with the calorie limit plotted as a reference line

acceptanceCriteria: A chart is displayed showing calorie consumption for the 7 most recent logged days (or the last 7 calendar days)
Each bar/point accurately reflects the total calories consumed for that date from the backend
A horizontal reference line is drawn at the current day's calorie limit value
The chart updates when food entries are added or deleted without requiring a page reload
Days with no logged data are shown as zero or clearly distinguished

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All backend logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo
Use React Query for all data fetching and mutations; invalidate relevant queries after mutations
Charts must be implemented using a library compatible with React (e.g., Recharts) — no external API calls
nonGoals: Push/browser notifications for exceeded calorie limit
Editing food entry details (only deletion is in scope)
Changes to the Food Upload or History screens
